finding: pandoc export fetched file:///-/URL-embedded resources server-side from untrusted markdown (SSRF + local-file-read); fixed with --sandbox
conversation_to_markdown feeds raw user/LLM chat text (incl. image syntax) into pandoc export, the SSRF source; mitigated by --sandbox on the converter
spawn_blocking pandoc could not be cancelled on timeout -> hung process + thread leak; switched to tokio::process + kill_on_drop
append_version double-emitted sync:file + double-spawned RAG reindex (commit_new_version already does both); removed the redundant calls
deliverables list did not preserve documented derived-union-pinned order (id=ANY load unordered); re-sort in Rust
shared FilePreviewDrawer swaps file prop without remount; a stale editor could Save file A content onto file B; fixed with editing reset on file.id + key on FileEditBody
Reload latest remounted editor with pre-fetch stale text (Plate never rebuilds on value prop); banner cleared -> save over stale content; fixed via setText(null) then remount
Cancel discarded unsaved edits with no confirmation (inconsistent with beforeunload guard); added confirm-on-dirty
false 'changed elsewhere' banner flashed during the user's own save (head bumps before onDone); suppressed while saving
load() permission-gate returned without caching, so getForConversation re-scheduled a no-op every render; cache empty on denial
reviewed ownership scoping on deliverables/append/export handlers — all correctly scoped to authenticated user_id; cross-user resolves to 404
reviewed append_version concurrency — commit_new_version's SELECT..FOR UPDATE row lock serializes writers; append-only, nothing lost
store mirrors Citations/FileVersions defineStore + sync-gated refetch conventions
integration tests exercise the real endpoints (append bump/noop/404, export md/docx/pdf/html) against a real server + DB — not cosmetic mocks
menu items are role=button + tabIndex + keydown; controls have aria-labels; passes the check:testid + gate lints

On getTextContent() failure the catch silently does setText('') with no error surfaced and loadedHeadRef stays null; the canvas then mounts an empty editor with no 'changed elsewhere' banner, so a user who makes any edit and Saves appends a near-empty head version that effectively clobbers the real (still-fetchable) content.

Plate's <Plate onChange> fires on selection/cursor changes too, not just content edits, so merely clicking into the markdown canvas sets dirty=true — enabling Save, arming the beforeunload guard, and forcing the 'Discard unsaved changes?' confirm on Cancel even when nothing was actually edited.

src_ext = file.filename.rsplit('.').next().unwrap_or("md") returns the whole filename for an extensionless file (rsplit always yields at least one item, so the unwrap_or fallback is unreachable); exporting an extensionless markdown deliverable writes input.<filename> and pandoc cannot infer a reader, failing the export with a 500.

pin_deliverable takes body: Option<Json<PinDeliverableRequest>>; axum's Option<Json<T>> yields None on ANY extractor rejection (invalid JSON or wrong Content-Type), so a client intending pinned=false with a malformed/mis-typed body is silently treated as pinned=true instead of returning 400.

Diff defines 6 new backend operations (File.listDeliverables/pinDeliverable/unpinDeliverable/export/appendVersion, Chat.exportConversation) and a new SyncEntity::Deliverable, and the frontend calls ApiClient.File.listDeliverables/pinDeliverable/unpinDeliverable etc., but the diff contains NO regeneration of ui/openapi/openapi.json, api-client/types.ts, or the desktop client. These ApiClient methods are undefined at runtime and tsc --noEmit fails; the emit_ts types_ts_parity golden test also fails once openapi.json is regenerated. Feature is non-functional/build-broken as committed.

serializeCsv/quoteCsvField deliberately does NOT neutralize formula-looking cells (=, +, -, @) for 'edit fidelity'. A CSV edited via CsvGridEditor is persisted verbatim by append_version and the plain Download button returns the raw bytes; opening such a file in Excel/Sheets executes the formula (CSV/formula injection, e.g. =cmd|'/C calc'!A1). The comment concedes the viewer's export serializer neutralizes but this save path does not.

pin_deliverable takes body: Option<Json<PinDeliverableRequest>>; axum resolves a malformed/invalid-JSON body to None (not a 400), so a client that intends pinned=false but mis-serializes the body silently gets pinned=true (default_true) instead of an error, hiding vs promoting a deliverable incorrectly.

<Plate onChange={() => onDirty?.()}> fires on selection/focus-only changes, not just content edits — clicking into the markdown canvas marks it dirty, enabling Save and arming the beforeunload + Cancel 'Discard unsaved changes?' guard even when nothing was edited. Should gate on an actual value change (onValueChange / content compare).

getContent returns raw serializeMd(editor); the normalizeMarkdown util built to keep saves byte-stable is never wired into the save path (zero non-test usages). Any edit re-serializes the whole document (e.g. '-' bullets rewritten to '*', whitespace normalized), so a one-word change yields a whole-document diff in FileVersionDiff and an edit-free save writes a gratuitously different new version instead of the intended no-op.

Export menu items are raw <div role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick/onKeyDown> inside a Popover instead of kit menu/Button components — violates the repo's no-raw-interactive-DOM kit convention (biome guardrail) that every other control in this diff follows.

PlateContent renders a role="textbox" editable region with only a placeholder and data-testid — no aria-label. The runtime a11y-name gate flags interactive edit regions with no accessible name (placeholder is not an accessible name); screen-reader users get an unnamed editor.
CodeMirror renders its editable as role="textbox" with no aria-label — the code canvas edit region has no accessible name, tripping the same a11y-name gate category as the markdown editor.

Icon-only kit Button rendered without size="icon" (icon passed as children at default size) — produces a mis-sized, non-square button with text padding around a lone icon; kit convention (see MarkdownToolbar in this diff) is size="icon" for icon-only buttons.
Export trigger is an icon-only kit Button (Download icon as children) without size="icon" — same non-square/mis-padded rendering; should use size="icon" per the kit icon-button convention.

The entire deliverables surface — list_deliverable_files (derived ∪ pinned − hidden set logic + ordering), pin_deliverable, unpin_deliverable, ownership-scoping and the sync emit — has ZERO integration tests. tests/file/artifacts_test.rs only covers append_version (3 tests) and file export (1 test); tests/file/mod.rs registers nothing else. The core curation/derivation logic (the actual feature) is unverified: a regression in the hidden/pinned merge or the derived SQL join would ship green.

conversation_to_markdown's doc-comment claims it is 'Pure + unit-tested per content-block variant', but there is no #[cfg(test)] module in export.rs and no integration test hits GET /conversations/{id}/export. The per-variant serialization (tool_use/tool_result/thinking/file_attachment/image field-probing with fallbacks) is entirely untested despite the explicit claim — a mis-parsed variant key would silently drop content from every export.

The added --sandbox flag on convert_to (and convert_to_pdf) is documented as a SECURITY boundary against SSRF / local-file-read via untrusted markdown (e.g. ![x](file:///etc/passwd), IMDS URLs), but no test asserts the boundary holds — no test feeds a file://- or http-embedded resource and verifies its bytes are NOT embedded in the output. A future pandoc arg refactor could drop --sandbox undetected.

The concurrent-edit safety logic (changedUnderneath: compares loadedHeadRef vs reactive currentHead, suppressed during own save, with Reload-latest/Keep-mine branches) is the most complex behavior in the feature yet is covered only by runtime-health render smoke (seeded-artifact-canvas-edit-body renders once). No integration/e2e drives an actual edit→save→append, a head advancing underneath, or the reload-remount path — so the banner's core race handling is unverified.

normalizeMarkdown is documented as 'Used on save so repeated saves of unchanged content produce byte-identical output (stable, minimal diffs)', and markdownRoundtrip.test.ts asserts its idempotency — but nothing in the diff calls it. The save path (FileEditBody → appendVersion) sends serializeMd(editor) directly, so the claimed stable-diff-on-save behavior is not actually wired; the unit test validates an unused code path rather than the real save flow.

CsvGridEditor parses the CSV with NO row cap (NO_CAP = MAX_SAFE_INTEGER, per the 'no data-loss' comment) and then renders one controlled kit <Input> per cell across the full grid with no virtualization (grid.rows.map → grid.headers.map → <Input>). A model-authored CSV with thousands of rows × tens of columns mounts tens of thousands of controlled inputs, freezing the tab — the read-only tabular viewer caps rows precisely to avoid this; the editable grid removes the cap AND uses heavier per-cell inputs.

setCell clones the ENTIRE rows matrix on every keystroke (g.rows.map(row => row.slice())), and setHeader/addRow/deleteRow likewise rebuild full arrays. Combined with the unvirtualized full-grid re-render, a single character typed in one cell reclones O(rows×cols) and re-renders every Input — quadratic-feeling latency on any non-trivial CSV.

The derived-deliverables query joins branch_messages on bm.message_id = fv.source_message_id and files, but migration 133 adds an index only on conversation_deliverables(file_id). If file_versions.source_message_id has no index (not added here), each deliverables list request scans file_versions for the conversation join — run on every FilePanel open and every sync:deliverable refetch. Verify an index backs the source_message_id join.

The two export menus label the same 6 formats inconsistently: the chat export extension uses 'Export as Word (.docx)' / 'Export as ODT' / 'Export as RTF' / 'Export as HTML', while FileExportMenu uses 'Word (.docx)' / 'OpenDocument (.odt)' / 'Rich Text (.rtf)' / 'HTML'. Success toasts also diverge ('Conversation exported as X' vs 'Exported as X'). Same feature, same formats, two vocabularies — reads as unpolished and complicates any future translation/testid alignment.
